heading: Release of citizens imprisoned by foreign governments
label: 1732 Release of citizens imprisoned by foreign governments
text: Whenever it is made known to the President that any citizen of the United States has been unjustly deprived of his liberty by or under the authority of any foreign government, it shall be the duty of the President forthwith to demand of that government the reasons of such imprisonment; and if it appears to be wrongful and in violation of the rights of American citizenship, the President shall forthwith demand the release of such citizen, and if the release so demanded is unreasonably delayed or refused, the President shall use such means, not amounting to acts of war and not otherwise prohibited by law, as he may think necessary and proper to obtain or effectuate the release; and all the facts and proceedings relative thereto shall as soon as practicable be communicated by the President to Congress.
notes: CodificationR.S. § 2001 derived from , .act July 27, 1868, ch. 249, § 315 Stat. 224Section was formerly classified to , Aliens and Nationality.section 903b of Title 8
Amendments1989— inserted “and not otherwise prohibited by law” after “acts of war”.Pub. L. 101–222
